# use-watcher-map

> React state management with path-based subscriptions for fine-grained re-renders. TypeScript-first. React 19+. Zero dependencies.

## Install

npm install use-watcher-map

## Core APIs

### useWatcherMap<T>(initialState): WatcherMap<T>
React hook. For nested object state. Call in a parent component, pass the watcher to children.
Methods: getState(), getPath(path), setState(state), setPath(path, value), clearPath(path), usePath(path), useState(), watchPath(path, fn), watchState(fn), batch(fn)

### useWatcher<T>(initialValue): WatcherPrimitive<T>
React hook. For primitive/simple values.
Methods: getState(), setState(value), useState(), watchState(fn)

### watcherStore<T>(initialState): WatcherStore<T>
NOT a hook. Module-level global store. Same API as useWatcherMap + onMount(fn).

## Path System

Dot-notation strings: "user.name", "todos.0.completed", "settings.theme"
PathOf<T> generates all valid paths as a string union (TypeScript autocomplete).
TypeAtPath<T, P> gives the type of the value at a path (type-safe setPath).

## Key Pattern

```tsx
const watcher = useWatcherMap<{ user: { name: string }; count: number }>({
  user: { name: 'Alice' },
  count: 0,
});

// In child component — only re-renders when user.name changes:
const name = watcher.usePath('user.name');

// Update — only notifies subscribers of user.name:
watcher.setPath('user.name', 'Bob');

// Batch multiple updates, notify once:
watcher.batch(() => {
  watcher.setPath('user.name', 'Bob');
  watcher.setPath('count', 1);
});
```

## Types

WatcherMap<T>, WatcherPrimitive<T>, WatcherStore<T> — return types of the three APIs.
PathOf<T> — union of all valid dot-notation paths.
TypeAtPath<T, P> — type at a given path.
